reasoning: The trigger requires compilerOptions.paths as a JSON array — invalid per the external tsconfig schema, so no real project reaches the state; even then the sole consequence is injecting a no-op tsconfig-paths/register hook (javascript-debug-adapter.ts:483-486). The test pins the behavior deliberately with an explanatory comment (edge2.test.ts:98-102), which the latent_bug rubric reads as a verification-role guard. Fails Reality: unreachable input, harmless effect. [Sweep-era expected.json was internally contradictory (verdict confirmed, body argued FP); panel 2-of-2 dismisses.]
gray_reason: The test's own title ('string/array treated as false', edge2.test.ts:88) contradicts the array assertion at :102, and the declared TsConfig type (config-transformer.ts:47) says paths is a Record — a confirm-at-info '!Array.isArray' hardening reading is defensible.
